children.name: Bufo
children.description: The archetypal toads of Eurasia — warty, terrestrial, slow-moving, and largely nocturnal. The Common Toad is Sweden's only toad, gathering in vast numbers at traditional breeding ponds each spring, often returning to the exact pond where it was born. Toxic parotoid glands behind the eyes deter most predators. Unlike frogs, toads walk rather than hop.

children.name: Bufo bufo
children.description: The quintessential Swedish toad; migrates hundreds of metres to natal pond in early spring; toxic parotoid glands produce bufadienolide poisons that deter most predators; male smaller than female and clasps her for days during amplexus.

children.name: Bufo spinosus
children.description: Recently separated from B. bufo; distinguished by more prominent dorsal tubercles; SW Europe.

children.name: Bufo gargarizans
children.description: Commonest toad across East Asia; skin secretions used in traditional Chinese medicine.

children.name: Anaxyrus
children.description: The New World radiation of true toads.

children.name: Anaxyrus americanus
children.description: Familiar garden visitor across eastern North America; musical trill lasts up to 30 seconds.

children.name: Anaxyrus boreas
children.description: Declining in western mountain lakes due to chytrid fungus; once aggregated in vast numbers.

children.name: Rhinella marina
children.description: Catastrophically invasive in Australia, Hawaii, and the Caribbean; toxic skin kills native predators; females lay up to 35,000 eggs per clutch; a cautionary tale in biocontrol.

children.name: Epidalea calamita
children.description: A narrow yellow dorsal stripe and the loudest call of any European amphibian, audible for kilometres; runs rather than hops; rare in southernmost Sweden; rapidly drying breeding pools allow it to outcompete other species.
